title: Terminal hardtanh direct output
hypothesis: With accepted lower-layer HardTanh grounding in place, bounded terminal HardTanh vec-op tails on fixed nm1 should benefit from writing directly to Y_DRAM instead of staging through activation SRAM.

direct_comparison.primary_question: Does bounded terminal HardTanh direct vec-op output improve latency and energy on fixed nm1 against the non-fused baseline?
include: one bounded HardTanh measurement baseline on fixed nm1
one paired direct-output candidate on the same model suite
exclude: broad nonlinear-family ranking beyond HardTanh in the first pass
full npu_top physical claims beyond the accepted reduced HardTanh proxy grounding
follow_on_broad_sweep: optional wider nonlinear-family sweep only after the bounded HardTanh comparison is accepted

expected_benefit: reduced terminal writeback latency for HardTanh vec-op tails
lower energy from removing a redundant SRAM write/read pair
risks: HardTanh perf gains may be smaller than tanh on the chosen bounded suite
reduced proxy physical grounding is sufficient for this staged proof but not for broad full-top ranking

expected_result.reason: first collect non-fused legality and reference metrics on the bounded HardTanh suite
direct vec-op output should remove the final activation-SRAM writeback roundtrip on the bounded HardTanh suite
